# substreams-websocket

> Real-time WebSocket fan-out server for Substreams `DatabaseChanges`. Decodes
> blocks from any `db_out`-style Substreams package and broadcasts them as JSON
> to connected WebSocket subscribers. URL conventions mirror Binance market
> streams: stream selectors are `<network>@<table>` (where `<table>` is the
> DatabaseChanges table emitted by the spkg's `db_out` — e.g. `swaps`,
> `transfers`, `spl_transfers`) with `*` wildcards on either side.

## Connecting

- `wss://<host>/ws/<network>@<table>` — single channel, raw payloads.
- `wss://<host>/ws/<a>/<b>/...` — multi channel, payloads wrapped as `{"stream":"<id>","data":<raw>}`.
- `wss://<host>/stream?streams=<a>/<b>/...` — query mode, always wrapped.
- Bare `/ws` returns HTTP 400; use `/ws/*@*` to subscribe to every (network, table) channel.

Clients subscribe by `<network>@<table>` where `<table>` is a DatabaseChanges table emitted by the spkg's `db_out` module (`swaps`, `transfers`, `spl_transfers`, ...).

## Block payload

```
{
  "network":     "solana-mainnet",
  "table":       "swaps",
  "block_num":   350000000,
  "block_hash":  "Gsk6...",
  "timestamp":   "2026-05-13 17:00:00",
  "module_hash": "bd388f2e...",
  "events": [
    { "input_amount": "1287000000", ... }
  ]
}
```

One payload per `(network, table)` group per block. All event field values are strings on the wire. The per-event `@table` prefix is dropped because the parent payload already carries `table` at the top level. On reconnect, pass `?from_timestamp=<n>` (Unix epoch seconds or UTC `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`) to receive every retained block with `timestamp_seconds > n`. Default retention is 600 seconds per spkg; outside that window the server emits a `gap` lifecycle message and clients must backfill via Substreams gRPC directly.

## Live commands

Send a JSON text frame on the connected socket:

```
{ "method": "SUBSCRIBE",         "params": ["solana-mainnet@swaps"], "id": 1 }
{ "method": "UNSUBSCRIBE",       "params": ["solana-mainnet@swaps"], "id": 2 }
{ "method": "LIST_SUBSCRIPTIONS", "id": 3 }
{ "method": "SET_FILTER",        "params": ["solana-mainnet@swaps", {"protocol":"raydium_cpmm","user":["a","b"]}], "id": 4 }
{ "method": "CLEAR_FILTER",      "params": ["solana-mainnet@swaps"], "id": 5 }
{ "method": "LIST_FILTERS",      "id": 6 }
```

Replies are `{"result": null | [...] | {...}, "id": <echoed>}`. Errors are `{"error": "...", "id": <echoed>}` and do not close the socket.

## Event filters

Reduce bandwidth by dropping non-matching events server-side. Pass `?filter=<url-encoded-json>` on the upgrade, or use `SET_FILTER` mid-stream. String equality only; fields AND, values OR; missing-field=miss; zero matches = block skipped. Wildcard selectors never filter. Full command and semantics reference is in `/SKILL.md`.

## Railway setup (quick reference)

Use these required env vars in Railway:

```
SUBSTREAMS_API_KEY=<pinax key>
SUBSTREAMS_AUTH_URL=https://auth.pinax.network/v1/auth/issue
SUBSTREAMS_WEBSOCKET_LISTEN=0.0.0.0:8080
SUBSTREAMS_WEBSOCKET_STREAMS_YAML=<inline YAML>
```

`SUBSTREAMS_WEBSOCKET_STREAMS_YAML` should contain the entire `streams.yaml` content as a multi-line value. On Railway, this env var should be preferred over file-path config because the filesystem is ephemeral.

Operational defaults:
- Set Railway Healthcheck Path to `/healthz`.
- If you need resume across redeploys, mount a volume (for example `/data/cursors`) and set `SUBSTREAMS_WEBSOCKET_CURSORS_DIR=/data/cursors`.
- For Solana-heavy traffic, plan enough memory headroom and use client message-size limits of at least 32 MiB.

## Troubleshooting (Railway + clients)

- Build diagnostics before consumers: verify `GET /healthz` returns `200 ok`, then check `GET /streams` to confirm your `network` + `table` exist before opening WebSocket consumers.
- Python clients must explicitly include `websockets` in `requirements.txt`; missing it typically fails with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'websockets'`.
- WebSocket client libraries rename connection kwargs across versions (for example `extra_headers` vs `additional_headers`). If you see `TypeError: ... unexpected keyword argument ...`, check your installed client version and update the argument name.
- Busy Solana blocks can exceed conservative default client frame/message limits (often ~1 MiB). Raise the client max frame/message size to at least `32 MiB` (for example `max_size=32 * 1024 * 1024`) to avoid silent disconnects or close code `1009`.
- If Railway logs are delayed, rely on `/healthz` and lifecycle messages (`"type":"stream","status":"error|fatal"`) as first-line diagnostics to cut time-to-fix.

## Resources

- [SKILL.md](/SKILL.md): full agent-oriented reference.
- `/`: interactive in-browser client (Scalar-style API reference + try-it panel).
